title: Fix unauthorized admin login error — allow username/password authentication for admin panel access

requirements.text: Fix the backend admin authentication so that username and password credentials are sufficient to grant admin access. The backend `loginAdmin` (or equivalent) function must accept a username+password pair and return a valid admin session/role without requiring Internet Identity or any additional principal-based check. Admin role must persist for the duration of the session.

quotes: solve the error of unauthorised admin login at the back-end
Username and password details are enough to make changes and upload images in website through admin panel

acceptanceCriteria: Calling the backend login function with the correct username and password hash returns a successful admin authorization result (not an 'Unauthorized' error).
All admin-gated backend functions (artwork upload, image upload, content edits) succeed when called after a valid username/password login without requiring an Internet Identity principal.
Incorrect credentials still return an authorization error.

requirements.text: Update the frontend admin authentication flow so that after a successful username/password login, all subsequent backend calls to admin-restricted endpoints (upload artwork, edit content, upload images, save social/media contacts) are authorized correctly. Ensure the session token or credential established at login is passed along with every admin backend call.

quotes: Username and password details are enough to make changes and upload images in website through admin panel

acceptanceCriteria: After logging in with correct username and password on the AdminLogin page, the user is redirected to the AdminPanel without any 'Unauthorized' error.
Uploading artwork images, editing site media, and saving social/media contacts in the AdminPanel all succeed without triggering an unauthorized error.
The admin session created by username/password login is recognized by the backend for all admin operations.
If the session is expired or missing, the user is redirected to the login page.

constraints: Do not remove or replace the existing username/password-based login mechanism — it must remain the sole requirement for admin access.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor.
nonGoals: Adding Internet Identity as a requirement for admin login.
Changing the public-facing portfolio design or layout.
Adding new admin features beyond fixing existing upload and content editing functionality.
